rows.description: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -
rows.notes: Both extractors agreed on row type and no monetary amounts. The description from gemini-flash was chosen for more accurate transcription of 'Mess.' and '& Co.' after visual verification.
rows.section_header: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -

rows.description: Dividend on 4108.17.5 Cons. 3 p.Ct for ½ Year due Jan. 5.1797
rows.notes: Monetary amounts are in full consensus and clearly legible. The description is largely agreed upon. The term 'p.Ct' is visually clear in the image and retained in the transcription, as identified by gemini-flash's notes (which also noted the transcription choice to 'p.Cent'). The fraction '½' from gemini-flash is preferred for visual accuracy.
rows.section_header: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -
rows.english_description: Half-year dividend on 3% Consols due 5 January 1797.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year dividend on 3% Consols due 5 January 1797, amounting to £61 12s 7d.

rows.notes: Monetary amounts are in full consensus and clearly legible. Description is a combination of extractors with visual verification. The start '&s:' is preferred (from gemini-flash), 'p.Ct' is visually evident. The month 'Ap.' is preferred over 'Aug.' (gpt-5-mini's choice) based on visual inspection and similarity to 'Ap. 4' in the subsequent line. gpt-5-mini's note correctly identified this month as an ambiguous reading.
rows.section_header: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -
rows.english_description: Dividend on Consols at 4% due 5 April.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Dividend on Consols at 4% due 5 April, amounting to £10 18s 4d.

rows.description: mem. Ap. 4. Bot. 1000£ 3p.Ct Cons. at 50¾. see soluta vana -
rows.notes: Both extractors agreed on row type and no monetary amounts. Description derived primarily from gemini-flash, correcting 'p.Cent' to 'p.Ct' based on visual verification. gemini-flash had more accurate readings for 'Bot.', '¾', and 'soluta vana' compared to gpt-5-mini, which correctly noted faint and abbreviated text.
rows.section_header: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -
rows.english_description: Memorandum noting purchase of 3% Consols at a stated price on 4 April; see 'soluta vana'.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Memorandum noting purchase of £1000 of 3% Consols at a stated price on 4 April; see 'soluta vana'.

rows.description: Dividend on 5108.17.5 Cons. 3 p.Ct for ½ Year due July 5.1797
rows.notes: Monetary amounts are in full consensus and clearly legible. The description is largely agreed upon. The term 'p.Ct' is visually clear in the image and retained in the transcription, as identified by gemini-flash's notes. The fraction '½' from gemini-flash is preferred for visual accuracy.
rows.section_header: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -
rows.english_description: Half-year dividend on 3% Consols due 5 July.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year dividend on 3% Consols due 5 July, amounting to £76 12s 7d.

rows.notes: Monetary amounts are in full consensus and clearly legible. Description is a combination of extractors with visual verification. The initial characters are '&s:', and 'p.Ct' is visually evident. The symbol before 'Oct. 10' is transcribed as 'D.', aligning with context (similar to 'D.due' in Row 3). gpt-5-mini noted the ambiguity of this symbol, which was helpful.
rows.section_header: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -
rows.english_description: Dividend on Consols at 4% due 10 October.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Dividend on Consols at 4% due 10 October, amounting to £10 18s 4d.

rows.description: Exchequer Annuity a Year to July 5
rows.notes: Monetary amounts and row type are in full consensus and clearly legible. Description had a minor difference in capitalization of 'Year'; gemini-flash is visually more accurate with 'Year' capitalized.
rows.section_header: Received by Mess. Biddulph Cocks, & Co. Bankers in London -
rows.english_description: Annual Exchequer annuity payable to 5 July.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual Exchequer annuity payable to 5 July, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Row type and description are in consensus. Monetary amounts differed significantly. Visual inspection confirms '210 / 10' as a common historical shorthand for £210 10s 0d. `gemini-flash` correctly interpreted this notation. `gpt-5-mini`'s interpretation of '1s 10d' is inconsistent with this convention and the visual grouping of the '10' as a single unit.
